commit.message:
Fix toolbar About button not appearing

AddAction's 4th arg is toolTipText (invisible), 5th is the visible
text label. "About" was only passed as the tooltip so the button
rendered with no icon and no text. Pass it for both args.

Fix left alignment: CSS container class with width: 88%, max-width: 1100px

Replaced all Tailwind padding-based containers with a bulletproof
CSS .via-container class that uses percentage-based width (88%) and
max-width (1100px). This guarantees 6% margin on each side at every
screen size — ultra-wide, laptop, tablet, and mobile. The previous
approach using Tailwind utility padding classes was not producing
visible inset from the screen edge.

Compléter tous les CRUDs frontend MVP (Élèves, Classes, Personnel, Parents, Paramètres)

État actuel MVP Frontend :
✅ Phase 1 (Foundation) : Dashboard, Login, Layout
✅ Phase 2 (Core Pédagogique) : Absences, Notes, Bulletins
✅ CRUD complet avec state local pour tous les modules

Modals créés/modifiés :
- AddStudentModal : mode Add/Edit avec onSubmit et student props
- AddParentModal : création complète avec tous champs (nom, contacts, profession)
- AddClassModal : mode Add/Edit avec niveau, capacité, titulaire, salle
- AddStaffModal : mode Add/Edit avec rôle dynamique (enseignant/admin)
- AddMatiereModal : gestion matières avec coefficient et couleur (10 prédéfinies + custom)
- AddTrimestreModal : gestion trimestres avec dates début/fin

CRUDs opérationnels :
- Élèves (students) : Add, Edit, Delete avec modal + state local

- Parents : Add, Edit, Delete avec enfants liés
- Paramètres/Settings :
  - Matières : Add, Edit, Delete avec coefficients et couleurs

Pattern CRUD appliqué partout :
- useState pour gérer collections (students, classes, staff, parents, etc.)
- Handlers : handleAdd, handleEdit, handleDelete avec confirm()
- Modals : isOpen/onClose + onSubmit callback + item pour edit
- useEffect dans modals pour populate form en mode edit
- Auto-increment IDs : array.length + 1

État des stats :
- Toutes les stats utilisent state dynamique (plus classesData/staffData en dur)
- Mise à jour temps réel lors ajout/suppression

Prochaine étape : Intégration Supabase (Phase 3)

- Auth et permissions
- Persistence des données

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce review token length from 32 to 16 characters to save SMS characters

🔧 URL Optimization:
- Changed from randomBytes(16) to randomBytes(8)
- Tokens now generate 16-character hex strings instead of 32
- Shorter URLs save SMS character space and costs

📱 SMS Benefits:
- Shorter review links in messages
- More room for personalized content
- Reduced SMS character count

🔒 Security:
- 8 bytes (64 bits) still provides strong collision resistance
- 16 hex characters remain unguessable for security

⚡ Examples:
- Old: app.growourreviews.com/review/ead1c739a545c3797343c7550126c6c2
- New: app.growourreviews.com/review/3f2a8b9c1d4e5f67

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Score import: ESPN adapter, authoritative winner derivation, historical backfill (#75)

* Add tournament games: normalized game model, score recording, and game generation

Backend:
- Add core.tournament_games table with bracket_position, round, region tracking
- Add tournament format column to tournaments table
- Add TournamentGame model and SQLC queries (CRUD, list by tournament/round)
- Add TournamentGameRepository with full adapter implementation
- Add game generator that creates tournament games from bracket structure
- Extend bracket service with dual-write (select winner writes to both bracket
  and tournament_games), score recording, and game generation endpoints
- Add HTTP handlers: list games, get game, record score, generate games
- Add tournament game DTO with response mapping
- Add integration tests for repository and unit tests for generator/service/DTOs

Frontend:
- Add tournament games query keys (games, gameDetail)
- Add tournamentGame schema with Zod validation
- Add bracket service methods for game listing and score recording
- Enhance BracketGameCard and CompactGameCard with score display
- Add games section to TournamentViewPage
- Update useBracket hook with game-aware queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix generate-recaps build: pass gameRepo to bracket.New after signature change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extract TournamentGameRepository, auto-derive winner from scores, and display game status

- Extract TournamentGameRepository from TournamentRepository so game
  CRUD has its own dedicated struct and wiring
- Auto-derive winner in RecordScore when no winner is set, triggering
  the full SelectWinner flow (wins, elimination, propagation)
- Use COALESCE in UpdateTournamentGameResult to preserve existing scores
  when NULL is passed (fixes score wipe on dual-write)
- Add game status field to BracketGame model, DTO, and frontend schema
- Display "Final" and "Live" status badges in bracket UI cards
- Enqueue prediction refresh in recordScoreHandler when winner is derived

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add backfill-games CLI tool to materialize historical tournament games

Adds a CLI tool that reconstructs tournament_games rows for historical
tournaments (2016-2025) by reusing the bracket builder and result
application logic. Scores are placeholder (1-0) since historical scores
were never captured. Also fixes tournamentGameToCreateParams to pass
through scores instead of hardcoding nil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use tournament games as source of truth for bracket rendering

Replace the dual applyCurrentResults + score overlay with a single
applyTournamentGames pure function that reads winners, scores, status,
and team assignments directly from tournament game records. Falls back
to the old win/bye-counter approach when no games exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ESPN score import service, school aliases, and score-authoritative winner derivation

Introduces a score import pipeline that fetches real game scores and times
from ESPN's scoreboard API and records them against tournament games. Key
changes:

- ESPN adapter: HTTP client, response parsing, school name alias mapping
- Score import service: matches ESPN games to tournament games by team pairs,
  records scores chronologically, rebuilds game index after each recording
  to handle winner corrections that propagate to later-round slots
- RecordScore now derives winners from scores and corrects mismatches
  instead of rejecting them, making the import authoritative over
  hand-entered data
- Score import worker for polling during live tournaments
- Backfill-games safety: refuse to regenerate if games have recorded results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove obsolete test for inconsistent winner/score rejection

RecordScore now auto-derives the winner from scores rather than
rejecting inconsistent combinations, making this test invalid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add hash caching: isDeepFrozen, primitive LRU, WeakMap cache (#2986)

* Add hashing performance benchmark: legacy vs. canonical

Deno.bench suite comparing merkle-reference (legacy) and canonicalHash()
across small/medium/large objects, arrays, repeated subtrees, and 16KB
payloads. Tests both cached (same object identity) and fresh (new object
each iteration) scenarios.

Key results:
- Cached: legacy 66-59570x faster (WeakMap hits vs. no caching)
- Fresh: canonical 1-4x faster (single-pass SHA-256 vs. merkle tree)
- Sparse arrays: canonical only (legacy throws on holes)

Co-Authored-By: researcher-rio (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename hashing_bench.ts to hashing-bench.ts (kebab-case)

Cherry-picked from researcher-rio/hashing-bench, renamed to follow
the project's kebab-case file naming convention.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add hash caching: isDeepFrozen utility, primitive LRU, WeakMap cache

Task A: Add isDeepFrozen() to deep-freeze.ts -- general-purpose recursive
check with WeakMap caching and circular reference handling.

Task B: Add bounded LRU cache (50K entries) for primitive value hashes
(strings, numbers, booleans, bigints) in canonicalHash(). Pre-compute
constant hashes for null and undefined.

Task C: Add WeakMap identity cache for deep-frozen object hashes in
canonicalHash(). Only caches immutable objects (isDeepFrozen check);
mutable objects always recompute.

Tests: 20 new isDeepFrozen tests covering primitives, objects, arrays,
sparse arrays, circular references, and caching. 9 new canonicalHash
caching tests covering primitives, frozen objects, and mutable objects.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Cache frozen unclaimed-fact objects in unclaimedRef()

Reuse a frozen { the, of } object per distinct pair so that identity-based
hash caches (WeakMap in canonicalHash, merkle-reference internal WeakMap)
hit on repeated calls instead of re-hashing a fresh mutable object each
time. The existing unclaimedCache LRU in reference.ts is left intact for
now as a follow-up cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix isDeepFrozen: only cache true results, never false

An object that is unfrozen when first checked may be deep-frozen later.
Caching false would permanently return a stale result. Now only confirmed
deep-frozen objects are cached. Adds a regression test for the
check-then-freeze scenario.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #2986 review feedback (11 comments)

canonical-hash.ts:
- Extract computeHash() helper to avoid repeating hash computation logic.
- Precompute constant hashes for true and false (in addition to null/undefined).
- Dispatch on typeof with switch statement instead of if-chain.

deep-freeze.ts:
- Remove redundant undefined checks -- typeof already excludes undefined
  from the object branch.

test/canonical-hash-test.ts:
- Use assertStrictEquals (identity equality) for all caching tests to
  verify the same object is returned, not just equal values.
- Add true/false constant hash tests.

test/deep-freeze-test.ts:
- Add symbol primitive test for completeness.
- Add array-inside-object tests (frozen and unfrozen child array).
- Add proxy-based cache verification tests for both objects and arrays
  to prove caching skips property traversal on repeat calls.

test/hashing-bench.ts:
- Replace manual legacy path recreation with setCanonicalHashConfig()
  toggle, matching the pattern used by unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CI typecheck: annotate makeLargeNestedTree return type

The function returned `unknown`, which is not assignable to
`DefinedReferent`. Changed return type to `Record<string, unknown>`.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use BenchContext.start() to exclude config setup from timing

Move setCanonicalHashConfig() calls before b.start() so flag toggling
is not included in the measured portion. Remove wrapper functions in
favor of inline setup + refer() calls.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add resetCanonicalHashConfig() teardown after b.end() in benchmarks

Ensures config teardown is excluded from timing along with setup.
Every bench now follows: setup -> b.start() -> refer() -> b.end() -> reset.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove redundant type casts in canonicalHash switch block

TypeScript narrows the type correctly within switch cases, so the
explicit casts to string | number | bigint are unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add deep-frozen and {the,of} benchmark groups

Adds three new benchmark sections:
- Deep-frozen same-object: small flat, nested, array, object+arrays, large tree
- Deep-frozen fresh-object: same shapes freshly allocated+frozen each iteration
- Frozen {the,of} unclaimed-fact pattern: same-object and fresh variants

These benchmarks show the WeakMap cache benefit for frozen objects.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Record deepFreeze() results in isDeepFrozen cache

After deepFreeze() finishes freezing an object/array tree, record it in
the deepFrozenCache so subsequent isDeepFrozen() calls return true
immediately without re-traversing. This makes deepFreeze() + canonicalHash()
a fast path: freeze once, then all future hashes hit the WeakMap cache.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Deduplicate deepFreeze() cache+return with if/else

Refactor array vs non-array paths to use if/else, so the freeze,
cache set, and return happen once at the end instead of being
duplicated in each branch.

Co-Authored-By: coder-cedar (Claude Opus 4.6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: move PDF generation to server-side for reliability

Client-side @react-pdf/renderer required Node.js polyfills that
couldn't be fully shimmed in the browser. Moving PDF generation
to a tRPC endpoint eliminates all polyfill issues and works
natively in Node.js.

- Create shared/diagnostico.ts with shared types and getStatusInfo
- Move DiagnosticoPdfDocument to server/diagnosticoPdf.tsx
- Add diagnostico.generatePdf tRPC endpoint (server-side rendering)
- Client now calls server endpoint instead of generating locally
- Remove vite-plugin-node-polyfills and buffer (no longer needed)
- iOS Safari: still uses window.open for PDF download

feat: add expo-router tabs with 7 test screens

Replace single App.tsx with expo-router tab navigation. Each tab tests
a specific @react-three/native capability with clear visual feedback:

- Box: baseline rendering (mesh, geometry, material, useFrame, lights)
- Touch: pointer events (onClick, onPointerDown/Up/Move/Over/Out)
- Texture: procedural DataTexture + remote URL TextureLoader
- GLTF: model loading via three's GLTFLoader + useLoader
- Resize: canvas container cycling (full/half/small) with size reporting
- Mount: unmount/remount lifecycle with event logging
- Multi: 4 independent canvases in a 2x2 grid

Each screen uses ErrorBoundary + StatusBanner for pass/fail/loading state.

commit.message:
Add unified intake pipeline and classify-pending sweep cron

- POST /api/intake: single entry point for all data types (content URLs,
  entity records, RSS feeds, sync triggers, classification sweeps)
- POST /api/cron/classify-pending: daily sweep of ALL entity tables to
  catch anything that landed without classification
- vercel.json: add classify-pending cron at 11 AM CT
- CLAUDE.md: document full intake pipeline, cron schedule, data sources

Every intake path now ends with: data in table → classified across 16
dimensions → junction tables populated → logged to ingestion_log.
